[[Patrick Kaufusi]] (brother) }} }} '''Antonio Kaufusi''' (born 27 November 1984) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. An [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] and [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] international and [[Queensland state rugby league team|Queensland]] [[State of Origin]] representative {{rlp|pr}}, he played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], [[Melbourne Storm]], [[North Queensland Cowboys]] and the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and the [[London Broncos]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]].{{cite news|first=Dave|last=Donaghy|newspaper=The Herald Sun|title=Melbourne Storm prop Antonio Kaufusi heads for North Queensland|url=http://www.news.com.au/heraldsun/story/0,21985,24218408-11088,00.html|access-date=24 March 2009|date=21 August 2008}} ==Background== Kaufusi was born in [[Matahau]], [[Tonga]]. His junior club was Brothers in Bundaberg and he played rugby union for [[Nudgee College]] in Brisbane in 2002.{{cite news |title=Nudgee pair crack both the codes |first=Steve |last=Ricketts |work=Brisbane Courier Mail |date=6 September 2002 |via=EBSCOHost}} ==Playing career== === Melbourne Storm=== Kaufusi made his first-grade début in round 22 against the [[Bulldogs Rugby League Football Club|Bulldogs]] on 10 August 2003. In 2006 Kaufusi was called up to the Australian tri-nations squad after [[Reni Maitua]] received an ankle injury in the first test against New Zealand. He played at prop forward in [[2006 NRL Grand Final|his first Grand Final in 2006]], when the Storm were defeated by the [[Brisbane Broncos]], 15–8.{{cite web |url=https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2015/05/23/kaufusi-brings-up-game-150/ |title=Kaufusi Brings up Game 150 |publisher=Bulldogs |date=23 May 2015 |access-date=11 October 2022}} He played State of Origin for [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]], making his début in game one 2007. A serious knee injury prevented Kaufusi from participating in the Storm's victorious [[National Rugby League season 2007#Grand final|2007 premiership]] winning team.{{cite news|first=Dave|last=Donaghy|newspaper=The Herald Sun|title=Antonio Kaufusi trains to ease Grand Final pain|url=http://www.news.com.au/heraldsun/story/0,21985,22527375-11088,00.html|access-date=24 March 2009|date=4 October 2007}} He played in the [[2008 NRL Grand Final]] defeat by Manly.{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,24449388-23214,00.html|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121204185816/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,24449388-23214,00.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=2012-12-04|title=Grand final: As it happened|work=Fox Sports|date=2008-10-05|access-date=2008-10-05}} He played his first test for [[Tonga]] against the [[Kiwis (rugby league)|Kiwis]] starting in the front row in a [[World Cup]] warm up match. He was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Tonga|Tonga]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm|title=Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-08|access-date=2008-10-09| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20081013130431/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm| archive-date= 13 October 2008 | url-status= live}} ===Cowboys=== In 2009, the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] signed Kaufusi on a large contract.{{cite web |url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/591581/Cowboys-capture-Storm-prop-Kaufisi |title=Cowboys capture Storm prop Kaufisi |publisher=Stuff |date=31 January 2009 |access-date=28 December 2020}} His stint with the Cowboys was short though, being released a year later. ===Knights=== On 25 June 2010, Kaufusi was released from his contract with the North Queensland Cowboys to join the [[Newcastle Knights]] for the rest of 2010 and a further two years.{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/knights-sign-ex-test-prop-kaufusi/story-e6frexnr-1225884030563|title=Knights sign ex-Test prop Kaufusi|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2017-04-25}}{{cite web |url=http://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/article/2010/06/24/148771_cowboys.html |title=Tate signs with Cowboys | Townsville Bulletin Sport - Cowboys |website=www.townsvillebulletin.com.au |access-date=26 January 2022 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20120919055202/http://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/article/2010/06/24/148771_cowboys.html |archive-date=19 September 2012 |url-status=dead}}{{cite news| url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/knights-ride-to-the-rescue-of-kaufusi-20100619-ynuj.html| work=The Sydney Morning Herald| title=Knights ride to the rescue of Kaufusi| first=Adrian| last=Proszenko| date=20 June 2010}}{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/456526/stone-banks-on-kaufusi-revival/|title=Stone banks on Kaufusi revival|first=Brett|last=Keeble|date=20 June 2010|website=Theherald.com.au|access-date=2017-04-25}} He made a winning début for the Knights in round 18 against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]]. ===London Broncos=== On 18 August 2011, Kaufusi signed a 3-year deal with the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]] after the 2012 Knights coach, [[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]], released him from his contract a year early.{{cite news| url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/14573951 | work=BBC News| title=Harlequins sign Shane Rodney and Antonio Kaufusi | date=18 August 2011 |access-date=6 July 2022 }} ===Huddersfield Giants=== Kaufusi played for [[Huddersfield Giants]] in 2014. ===Bradford Bulls=== On 2 May 2014, Kaufusi was loaned to [[Bradford Bulls]] for the rest of the 2014 season.{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30123/bulls-take-kaufusi-on-loan |title=RUGBY-LEAGUE.COM | Rugby-League.com |website=Superleague.co.uk |access-date=2017-04-25}}{{cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12213/9293638/super-league-bradford-bulls-bring-in-prop-antonio-kaufusi-from-huddersfield-giants |title=Super League: Bradford Bulls bring in prop Antonio Kaufusi from Huddersfield Giants | Rugby League News |publisher=[[Sky Sports]] |access-date=2017-04-25}} ===Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs=== On 13 November 2014, Kaufusi signed a 2-year contract with the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] starting in 2015, to return to Australia.{{cite web|url=http://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2014/11/13/kaufusi_joins_the_bulldogs.html |title=Kaufusi
